content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

January 28, 2020

Rafael Menis
1150 Hazel St
Pinole CA 94564

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. 1-20-007

Dear Mr. Menis:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Because your questions are general in nature, we are treating
your inquiry as a request for informal assistance.²

Please note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1
FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for
additional advice.

QUESTION

May leftover campaign signs and business cards be used in another election if the committee
was terminated with surplus funds by reporting them as an in-kind contribution to a new
committee?

CONCLUSION

A committee may reuse leftover campaign yard signs and business cards for another election
so long as they were purchased by the prior committee with the intention of being used in the
previous campaign (i.e., they include candidate and committee information from previous
campaign) and are reported as an in-kind contribution to the new committee.

___________________________

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

File No. I-20-007
Page No. 2

FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER

You were a candidate and had a campaign committee that was terminated with surplus funds
in 2019. You have now opened a new committee and are seeking advice on whether you may reuse
leftover campaign yard signs and business cards from your terminated committee for your new
committee. You would like to know if it is permissible to give these leftover campaign materials as
an in-kind contribution to your new committee or if the leftover materials are also considered
surplus.

ANALYSIS

Sections 84307 and 89510 provide that campaign funds do not become the personal property
of the candidate. Campaign funds include any contributions, cash, cash equivalents, and other
assets received or possessed by a committee as defined by subdivision (a) of Section 82013.
(Section 89511.) You provided that your committee had leftover campaign signs and business cards
at the committee terminated.

Although it is likely the campaign yard signs and business cards originally purchased for the
committee had a fair market value at the time they were purchased, it is unlikely the campaign signs
and business cards had a value after the election ended, as they cannot be returned or used again for
another purpose other than for your election campaign. Thus, the value after the election of leftover
yard signs and business cards would be de minimis in the case that the yard signs are made of the
traditional cardstock and wooden or metal wire posts and were printed only for the use of your
campaign.

However, now that you have opened a new committee and are seeking to reuse the leftover
campaign yard signs and business cards for another election, the campaign signs and business cards
now have a value. Accordingly, the campaign yard signs and business cards may be reused and
must be reported as an in-kind contribution to the new committee by the prior committee. However,
the yard signs and business cards must have been intended for use by the prior committee,
purchased for the sole purpose of using them in the prior campaign, and must include candidate and
committee information from previous campaign. The in-kind contribution value would be the
purchase price of the yard signs and business cards at the time they were initially purchased by the
prior committee.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Dave Bainbridge
General Counsel

By: Katelyn Greene
Counsel, Legal Division

KG:sal

sections.question:
May leftover campaign signs and business cards be used in another election if the committee 
was terminated with surplus funds by reporting them as an in-kind contribution to a new 
committee?
sections.conclusion:
A committee may reuse leftover campaign yard signs and business cards for another election 
so long as they were purchased by the prior committee with the intention of being used in the 
previous campaign (i.e., they include candidate and committee information from previous 
campaign) and are reported as an in-kind contribution to the new committee.2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal 
written advice. (Section 83 I 14; Regulation l 8329(c)(3).)

Although it is likely the campaign yard signs and business cards originally purchased for the
